---
title: "data_overview"
author: "hwittich"
date: "2021-01-28"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Introduction
For this test run, I'm working with data from the AFA population group of the Trans-Omics for Precision Medicine (TOPMed) cohort. 

# Dosage File
We are starting with a dosage file, as formatted below:
```{r}
head <- c("CHR","SNP","BP","A1","A2","?","Fn","In")
head
```
Each row in the dosage file corresponds with a SNP. The "CHR" field lists the chromosome code, and the "BP" field contains the base-pair position of each SNP. The "SNP" field stores the SNP code, which is a concatenation of the chromosome code and base-pair location. The "A1" and "A2" fields list the allele 1 and allele 2 codes, respectively, and the "?" contains ??? a p-value? a quality metric? not sure. FInally, at the end of each line, there are n pairs of dosage values (?), where n is the number of genotyped individuals. The IDs for these individuals can be found in the samples.txt data file.

# Protein Expression File
Each row corresponds to a protein (we are only working with one at the moment) and each column corresponds to an individual.
